summary:
Fixed.

Problem  
Product.hashCode() returned category.hashCode(), so the 10 000 products were mapped into only 10 distinct hash values (one per category). All items in the same category landed in the same bucket, causing lookup time to degrade from O(1) to O(n).

Fix  
Use the SKU – the key used in equals() – for the hash.

src/Product.java

```java
@Override
public int hashCode() {
    // Use SKU because equality is defined by SKU.
    return sku.hashCode();
}
```

Result  
Running `test.sh` now passes:

```
=== Performance Cliff Hash Map Test ===
--- Small dataset (100 items) ---
...
Small dataset: OK
--- Hash distribution analysis ---
Max chain length: 4
OK: Max chain length 4 is within acceptable bounds
PASS: Hash map distributes items evenly across buckets
```

The hash map no longer suffers severe bucket collisions.
